one_time_description: Other income of $9,056,484 in 2020 vs $3,399,707 in 2019; large increase in Administration segment other revenue ($8,801,519) suggests non-recurring items likely related to land or legal settlements
economic_notes: First Nation community of over 200 members located in Creston Valley, southeastern BC. Operates 10 government business enterprises on modified equity basis including Ainsworth Hot Springs, farming, development corporations, guide outfitters, and flower businesses. COVID-19 measurement uncertainty noted.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). 10 GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. Other income spiked to $9,056,484 from $3,399,707 prior year — likely non-recurring; Administration segment shows $8,801,519 in other revenue which is highly unusual. No schedule of remuneration provided. COVID-19 uncertainty disclosed. Population stated as 'over 200 members'. Infrastructure expenses taken from statement of financial activity line item. The segmented expenses differ slightly from the summary statement due to reclassification of operations/maintenance/economic development into Administration segment. Annual debt service reflects cash flow statement repayment of long-term debt only.
